Weigh the v2 docs heavily when answering questions over the v1 docs.\n     6→\n     7→## Project Overview\n     8→\n     9→Flipt v2 is a Git-native feature management platform with a monorepo structure. This is the v2 version with a Git-native architecture.\n    10→\n    11→- **v1 code**: Located on `main` branch\n    12→- **v2 code**: Located on current (`v2`) branch. This is the default branch in the repository.\n    13→- **License**: Fair Core License for server, MIT for client code\n    14→- **Requirements**: Go 1.25+ and Node.js 18+\n    15→\n    16→## Project Architecture & Structure\n    17→\n    18→### Repository Structure\n    19→\n    20→- `cmd/flipt/` - Main application entry point\n    21→- `core/` - Core validation and business logic\n    22→- `errors/` - Shared error definitions\n    23→- `internal/` - Core application logic (not importable)\n    24→  - `cmd/` - Internal command utilities and code generation tools\n    25→  - `common/` - Shared types and utilities across internal packages\n    26→  - `config/` - Configuration management\n    27→  - `containers/` - Container test utilities\n    28→  - `coss/` - Commercial open source features (license management, enterprise storage, secrets)\n    29→  - `secrets/` - Secret management system with provider interface (OSS file provider, Pro Vault provider)\n    30→  - `credentials/` - Credential management for cloud storage and Git authentication\n    31→  - `ext/` - Import/export functionality for flags and segments\n    32→  - `gateway/` - HTTP gateway implementation\n    33→  - `info/` - Version and build information\n    34→  - `migrations/` - Database migration files (ClickHouse for analytics)\n    35→  - `otel/` - OpenTelemetry integration (logs, metrics, traces)\n    36→  - `product/` - Product metadata and feature flags\n    37→  - `release/` - Release version checking\n    38→  - `server/` - gRPC and HTTP server implementations\n    39→    - `analytics/` - Analytics collection server (Prometheus, ClickHouse)\n    40→    - `authn/` - Authentication server and middleware\n    41→    - `authz/` - Authorization server with Rego engine\n    42→    - `environments/` - Environment management server\n    43→    - `evaluation/` - Flag evaluation server with OFREP support\n    44→    - `metadata/` - Metadata API server\n    45→  - `storage/` - Storage abstractions and implementations\n    46→    - `analytics/` - Analytics data storage\n    47→    - `authn/` - Authentication storage (Redis, memory, static)\n    48→    - `environments/` - Environment-aware storage with Git support\n    49→    - `fs/` - File system abstraction layer\n    50→    - `git/` - Git repository management\n    51→  - `telemetry/` - Usage telemetry collection\n    52→- `rpc/` - Protocol buffer definitions and generated code\n    53→  - `flipt/` - Main Flipt API (v1)\n    54→  - `v2/` - V2 APIs (environments, analytics, evaluation)\n    55→- `sdk/` - Client SDKs (Go SDK included)\n    56→- `ui/` - React/TypeScript frontend\n    57→\n    58→### Key Components\n    59→\n    60→#### Storage Layer\n    61→\n    62→Multi-backend storage supporting:\n    63→\n    64→- Git repositories (primary for v2)\n    65→- File system (local development)\n    66→- Cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) - Flipt config only\n    67→\n    68→#### Environments System\n    69→\n    70→- Environment-per-branch: Map Git branches to environments\n    71→- Environment-per-directory: Organize by microservice/team\n    72→- Environment-per-repository: Separate repos for products\n    73→\n    74→#### APIs\n    75→\n    76→- gRPC API (port 9000) - Primary interface\n    77→- REST API (port 8080) - HTTP gateway over gRPC\n    78→- OpenFeature compatible evaluation\n    79→\n    80→##### Key Endpoints\n    81→\n    82→- **Evaluation API**: `/api/v1/evaluate` - Evaluate feature flags\n    83→- **Management API**: Full CRUD operations on flags, segments, rules\n    84→- **Analytics API**: `/api/v2/analytics` - Flag evaluation metrics\n    85→- **Environments API**: `/api/v2/environments` - Environment management\n    86→- **OFREP API**: `/ofrep/v1` - OpenFeature Remote Evaluation Protocol\n    87→\n    88→#### UI Architecture\n    89→\n    90→- React 19 with TypeScript\n    91→- Redux Toolkit for state management\n    92→- Tailwind CSS for styling\n    93→- Vite for build tooling\n    94→- React Router for navigation\n    95→\n    96→## Development Setup & Commands\n    97→\n    98→### Initial Setup\n    99→\n   100→```bash\n   101→# Install required development tools\n   102→mage bootstrap\n   103→```\n   104→\n   105→### Development Workflow\n   106→\n   107→1. **Setup**: Run `mage bootstrap` to install tools\n   108→2. **Backend Development**:\n   109→   - Use `mage go:run` for server\n   110→   - Server runs on port 8080 (HTTP) and 9000 (gRPC)\n   111→3. **Frontend Development**:\n   112→   - Run `mage ui:run` for UI dev server on port 5173\n   113→   - UI proxies API requests to backend on port 8080\n   114→4. **Full Development**: Run both servers simultaneously\n   115→\n   116→### Build & Run Commands\n   117→\n   118→- `mage build` - Builds the project similar to a release build (default target)\n   119→- `mage go:build` - Builds Go server for development without bundling assets\n   120→- `mage go:run` - Runs Go server in development mode with local config\n   121→- `mage dev` - Alias for go:run\n   122→- `./bin/flipt server --config config/local.yml` - Run built binary with local config\n   123→\n   124→### UI Development Commands\n   125→\n   126→- `mage ui:deps` - Install UI dependencies\n   127→- `mage ui:run` - Run UI in development mode (port 5173)\n   128→- `mage ui:dev` - Alias for ui:run\n   129→- `mage ui:build` - Build UI assets for release\n   130→- `cd ui && npm run dev` - Alternative way to run UI dev server\n   131→\n   132→### Testing Commands\n   133→\n   134→- `mage go:test` - Run all Go unit tests\n   135→- `go test -v {path} -run {test}` - Run a specific Go test\n   136→- `mage go:bench` - Run Go benchmarking tests\n   137→- `mage go:cover` - Run tests and generate coverage report\n   138→- `cd ui && npm run test` - Run UI unit tests (Jest)\n   139→\n   140→### Code Quality Commands\n   141→\n   142→- `mage go:lint` - Run Go linters (golangci-lint and buf lint)\n   143→- `mage go:fmt` - Format Go code with goimports\n   144→- `mage ui:lint` - Run UI linters (ESLint)\n   145→- `mage ui:fmt` - Format UI code (Prettier)\n   146→\n   147→### Code Generation Commands\n   148→\n   149→- `mage go:proto` - Generate protobuf files and gRPC stubs\n   150→- `mage go:mockery` - Generate mocks\n   151→- `mage go:generate` - Generate both mocks and proto files\n   152→\n   153→### Cleanup Commands\n   154→\n   155→- `mage clean` - Clean built files and tidy go.mod\n   156→- `mage prep` - Prepare project for building (clean + ui:build)\n   157→\n   158→### Build Process Notes\n   159→\n   160→- Protocol buffers generate Go and gateway code\n   161→- Mockery generates Go test mocks. See .mockery.yml\n   162→- UI has no code generation\n   163→\n   164→## Code Style Guidelines\n   165→\n   166→### Go Code Style\n   167→\n   168→#### Foundation: Google Go Style Guide\n   169→\n   170→**All Go code should follow the [Google Go Style Guide](https://google.github.io/styleguide/go/guide) as the base style guide.**\n   171→\n   172→The guidelines below are Flipt-specific conventions that build upon the Google style guide. When in doubt, refer to the Google guide first, then apply these project-specific patterns.\n   173→\n   174→Key areas from the Google guide to pay special attention to:\n   175→\n   176→- **Formatting**: Use `gofmt` (we use `goimports` which includes `gofmt`)\n   177→- **Naming**: Follow Go naming conventions for packages, types, functions, and variables\n   178→- **Package organization**: Keep packages focused and avoid circular dependencies\n   179→- **Error handling**: Return errors as the last return value, handle errors explicitly\n   180→- **Documentation**: Write clear godoc comments for exported types and functions\n   181→\n   182→#### Naming Conventions\n   183→\n   184→- **Public functions**: Use PascalCase (`NewServer`, `ListFlags`, `RegisterGRPC`)\n   185→- **Private functions**: Use camelCase (`getStore`, `buildConfig`, `handleError`)\n   186→- **Factory functions**: Use `New` prefix for constructors (`NewRepository`, `NewEnvironmentFactory`)\n   187→- **Variables**: Use camelCase (`environmentKey`, `namespaceKey`, `startTime`)\n   188→- **Constants**: Use PascalCase for exported, camelCase for unexported\n   189→- **Interfaces**: Use descriptive names (`Store`, `EnvironmentStore`, `Validator`)\n   190→\n   191→#### Variable Declarations\n   192→\n   193→```go\n   194→// Prefer := for single variable declarations\n   195→result := SomeType{}\n   196→\n   197→// Use var blocks for multiple related variables\n   198→var (\n   199→    environmentKey = r.EnvironmentKey\n   200→    namespaceKey   = r.NamespaceKey\n   201→    startTime      = time.Now().UTC()\n   202→)\n   203→```\n   204→\n   205→#### Constants\n   206→\n   207→Use constants when appropriate for values that are unlikely to change. Even in tests.\n   208→\n   209→**❌ Bad Examples**\n   210→\n   211→```go\n   212→var (\n   213→    maxRetries = 3\n   214→    secretProvider = \"vault\"\n   215→)\n   216→```\n   217→\n   218→```go\n   219→  maxRetries := 3\n   220→  secretProvider := \"vault\"\n   221→```\n   222→\n   223→**✅ Good Example**\n   224→\n   225→```go\n   226→const (\n   227→    maxRetries = 3\n   228→    secretProvider = \"vault\"\n   229→)\n   230→```\n   231→\n   232→#### Error Handling\n   233→\n   234→##### Error Types\n   235→\n   236→Use the custom error types when possible:\n   237→\n   238→```go\n   239→// Preferred - use custom error types\n   240→return nil, errs.ErrNotFoundf(\"flag %q not found\", key)\n   241→return nil, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"invalid configuration: %s\", reason)\n   242→\n   243→// Fallback - standard error handling\n   244→return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to process: %w\", err)\n   245→```\n   246→\n   247→##### Inline Errors\n   248→\n   249→Prefer inline error declarations and checking when it makes sense.\n   250→\n   251→**❌ Bad Example**\n   252→\n   253→```go\n   254→err := doSomething()\n   255→if err != nil {\n   256→    // ...\n   257→}\n   258→```\n   259→\n   260→**✅ Good Example**\n   261→\n   262→```go\n   263→if err := doSomething(); err != nil {\n   264→    // ...\n   265→}\n   266→```\n   267→\n   268→#### Imports\n   269→\n   270→Follow the three-group import pattern (extends Google guide):\n   271→\n   272→```go\n   273→import (\n   274→    // Standard library\n   275→    \"context\"\n   276→    \"fmt\"\n   277→    \"time\"\n   278→\n   279→    // Third-party packages\n   280→    \"github.com/go-git/go-git/v6\"\n   281→    \"go.uber.org/zap\"\n   282→\n   283→    // Local packages\n   284→    \"go.flipt.io/flipt/errors\"\n   285→    \"go.flipt.io/flipt/internal/config\"\n   286→)\n   287→```\n   288→\n   289→#### Logging\n   290→\n   291→Use structured logging with zap:\n   292→\n   293→```go\n   294→// Preferred - structured logging with context\n   295→s.logger.Debug(\"processing request\", \n   296→    zap.String(\"environment\", envKey),\n   297→    zap.String(\"namespace\", nsKey),\n   298→    zap.Int(\"count\", len(items)))\n   299→\n   300→// Use appropriate log levels\n   301→s.logger.Debug(\"debug info for development\")  // Most common\n   302→s.logger.Info(\"important application events\")\n   303→s.logger.Warn(\"recoverable error conditions\")\n   304→s.logger.Error(\"error conditions that need attention\")\n   305→\n   306→// Avoid overusing log statements - be selective about what to log\n   307→```\n   308→\n   309→#### Comments and Documentation\n   310→\n   311→```go\n   312→// Public functions need godoc comments starting with function name\n   313→// ListFlags lists all flags in the specified environment and namespace\n   314→func (s *Server) ListFlags(ctx context.Context, r *flipt.ListFlagRequest) (*flipt.FlagList, error) {\n   315→    // Inline comments explain complex logic\n   316→    // Check for X-Environment header for backward compatibility\n   317→    environmentKey := r.EnvironmentKey\n   318→    if headerEnv, ok := common.FliptEnvironmentFromContext(ctx); ok && headerEnv != \"\" {\n   319→        environmentKey = headerEnv\n   320→    }\n   321→}\n   322→```\n   323→\n   324→#### Project Organization\n   325→\n   326→- Use `internal/` for private packages that shouldn't be imported externally\n   327→- Organize by domain (`server/`, `storage/`, `config/`, `analytics/`)\n   328→- Keep interfaces in domain root, implementations in subdirectories\n   329→- Co-locate tests with implementation (`*_test.go`)\n   330→- Use `testdata/` directories for test fixtures\n   331→\n   332→#### Pre-commit Quality Checks (Go)\n   333→\n   334→**Always run these commands when adding or editing Go code:**\n   335→\n   336→```bash\n   337→# Format Go code\n   338→mage go:fmt\n   339→\n   340→# Lint Go code\n   341→mage go:lint\n   342→\n   343→# Run modernize to update code to 1.24+ style\n   344→mage go:modernize\n   345→```\n   346→\n   347→### UI/React/TypeScript Code Style\n   348→\n   349→#### File Naming and Organization\n   350→\n   351→- **Components**: Use PascalCase for `.tsx` files (`FlagForm.tsx`, `FlagTable.tsx`)\n   352→- **API files**: Use camelCase with suffix (`flagsApi.ts`, `authApi.ts`)\n   353→- **Types**: Use PascalCase for `.ts` files (`Flag.ts`, `Analytics.ts`)\n   354→- **Utilities**: Use descriptive lowercase names (`helpers.ts`)\n   355→\n   356→#### Component Structure\n   357→\n   358→- Use functional components with hooks\n   359→- Organize components by domain in directories\n   360→- Keep reusable UI components in `ui/` directory\n   361→- Co-locate component tests with implementation\n   362→\n   363→#### State Management\n   364→\n   365→- Use Redux Toolkit for application state\n   366→- Create API slices for domain-specific data (`flagsApi.ts`)\n   367→- Use TypeScript for type safety throughout\n   368→\n   369→#### Pre-commit Quality Checks (UI)\n   370→\n   371→**Always run these commands when adding or editing UI code:**\n   372→\n   373→```bash\n   374→# Format UI code (TypeScript/React)\n   375→mage ui:fmt\n   376→\n   377→# Lint UI code (ESLint)\n   378→mage ui:lint\n   379→```\n   380→\n   381→### General Pre-commit Checks\n   382→\n   383→```bash\n   384→# Format specific markdown files (replace with actual filename)\n   385→npx prettier -w README.md\n   386→```\n   387→\n   388→## Configuration\n   389→\n   390→### Configuration Files\n   391→\n   392→- `config/local.yml` - Local development config\n   393→- `config/dev.yml` - Alternative dev config\n   394→- User config at `{{ USER_CONFIG_DIR }}/flipt/config.yml`\n   395→\n   396→### Debugging Tips\n   397→\n   398→- Use `FLIPT_LOG_LEVEL=debug` for verbose logging\n   399→- Check `/metrics` endpoint for Prometheus metrics\n   400→- View OpenTelemetry traces for request flow\n   401→- Git sync issues: Check repository access and credentials\n   402→- Authentication issues: Verify redirect URLs and client configurations\n   403→\n   404→## Testing Guidelines\n   405→\n   406→### Test Structure\n   407→\n   408→- Go tests use testcontainers for unit tests that depend on external tools\n   409→- UI tests use Jest for unit tests and Playwright for E2E\n   410→\n   411→### Writing Effective Tests\n   412→\n   413→When writing tests, ensure they actually verify the functionality being tested:\n   414→\n   415→**❌ Bad Example - Test doesn't verify actual behavior:**\n   416→\n   417→```go\n   418→// This test only verifies the method runs without error, not that the logic works\n   419→func TestFeature(t *testing.T) {\n   420→    result, err := service.DoSomething(input)\n   421→    require.NoError(t, err)\n   422→    assert.NotNil(t, result)\n   423→}\n   424→```\n   425→\n   426→**✅ Good Example - Test verifies specific behavior:**\n   427→\n   428→```go\n   429→// This test verifies the actual logic and behavior\n   430→func TestFeature(t *testing.T) {\n   431→    // Use mock.MatchedBy to verify the correct data is passed through\n   432→    store.On(\"Method\", mock.MatchedBy(func(ctx context.Context) bool {\n   433→        // Verify the context contains the expected values\n   434→        value, ok := somePackage.ValueFromContext(ctx)\n   435→        return ok && value == expectedValue\n   436→    }), expectedArg).Return(expectedResult, nil)\n   437→    \n   438→    result, err := service.DoSomething(input)\n   439→    require.NoError(t, err)\n   440→    \n   441→    // Verify the actual business logic worked correctly\n   442→    assert.Equal(t, expectedOutput, result.ImportantField)\n   443→    store.AssertExpectations(t) // Ensures mocks were called as expected\n   444→}\n   445→```\n   446→\n   447→**Key Testing Principles:**\n   448→\n   449→- **Verify behavior, not just success**: Don't just check that methods return without error\n   450→- **Test the logic**: Use `mock.MatchedBy` to verify correct data flows through the system\n   451→- **Test edge cases**: Include tests for when conditions are met vs. not met\n   452→- **Use meaningful assertions**: Assert on the specific values that matter to the business logic\n   453→- **Mock verification**: Use `mock.AssertExpectations(t)` to ensure mocks were called with expected parameters\n   454→\n   455→### Integration Tests\n   456→\n   457→Integration tests are defined in `build/testing/` and run as Mage targets. ForwardFliptEnvironment middleware to v1 API gateway\n   504→- Added comprehensive test coverage\n   505→\n   506→Fixes #4411\"\n   507→\n   508→git commit -s -m \"fix: resolve race condition in flag evaluation cache\"\n   509→\n   510→git commit -s -m \"test: improve X-Environment header test coverage\n   511→\n   512→- Use mock.MatchedBy to verify context contains correct environment\n   513→- Add test for when header is not present\n   514→- Ensure tests actually validate the header precedence logic\"\n   515→```\n   516→\n   517→### Pull Request Guidelines\n   518→\n   519→#### PR Title description\n   537→- **Added `file2_test.go`**: Test coverage description\n   538→- **Updated `config.yml`**: Configuration change description\n   539→\n   540→## Backward Compatibility\n   541→\n   542→Describe any backward compatibility or breaking change considerations.\n   543→\n   544→## Additional Notes\n   545→\n   546→Any other context, screenshots, or related issues.\n   547→```\n   548→\n   549→#### PR Best Practices\n   550→\n   551→- **Keep PRs focused**: One logical change per PR\n   552→- **Write descriptive titles**: Use conventional commit format\n   553→- **Provide context**: Explain the \"why\" behind changes\n   554→- **Include tests**: Always add tests for new functionality\n   555→- **Run quality checks**: Format and lint before creating PR\n   556→- **Reference issues**: Use \"Fixes #123\" to auto-close issues\n   557→- **Review your own PR**: Look through the diff before requesting review\n   558→- **Base PRs on the correct branch**: Base PRs on the correct branch (e.g. `v2` for v2 changes, `main` for v1 changes)\n   559→- **Label PRs correctly**: When creating a feature based off of the v2 branch, label the PR with the `v2` label. Similarly for a v1 (main) feature, use the `v1` label.\n   560→\n   561→#### Quality Checklist\n   562→\n   563→Before creating a PR, ensure:\n   564→\n   565→- [ ] Code follows style guidelines\n   566→- [ ] Tests are added and passing\n   567→- [ ] Linting passes (`mage go:lint` / `mage ui:lint`)\n   568→- [ ] Code is formatted (`mage go:fmt` / `mage ui:fmt`)\n   569→- [ ] Commit messages are clear and descriptive\n   570→- [ ] PR description explains the change and its purpose\n   571→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Weigh the v2 docs heavily when answering questions over the v1 docs.\n\n## Project Overview\n\nFlipt v2 is a Git-native feature management platform with a monorepo structure. This is the v2 version with a Git-native architecture.\n\n- **v1 code**: Located on `main` branch\n- **v2 code**: Located on current (`v2`) branch. This is the default branch in the repository.\n- **License**: Fair Core License for server, MIT for client code\n- **Requirements**: Go 1.25+ and Node.js 18+\n\n## Project Architecture & Structure\n\n### Repository Structure\n\n- `cmd/flipt/` - Main application entry point\n- `core/` - Core validation and business logic\n- `errors/` - Shared error definitions\n- `internal/` - Core application logic (not importable)\n  - `cmd/` - Internal command utilities and code generation tools\n  - `common/` - Shared types and utilities across internal packages\n  - `config/` - Configuration management\n  - `containers/` - Container test utilities\n  - `coss/` - Commercial open source features (license management, enterprise storage, secrets)\n  - `secrets/` - Secret management system with provider interface (OSS file provider, Pro Vault provider)\n  - `credentials/` - Credential management for cloud storage and Git authentication\n  - `ext/` - Import/export functionality for flags and segments\n  - `gateway/` - HTTP gateway implementation\n  - `info/` - Version and build information\n  - `migrations/` - Database migration files (ClickHouse for analytics)\n  - `otel/` - OpenTelemetry integration (logs, metrics, traces)\n  - `product/` - Product metadata and feature flags\n  - `release/` - Release version checking\n  - `server/` - gRPC and HTTP server implementations\n    - `analytics/` - Analytics collection server (Prometheus, ClickHouse)\n    - `authn/` - Authentication server and middleware\n    - `authz/` - Authorization server with Rego engine\n    - `environments/` - Environment management server\n    - `evaluation/` - Flag evaluation server with OFREP support\n    - `metadata/` - Metadata API server\n  - `storage/` - Storage abstractions and implementations\n    - `analytics/` - Analytics data storage\n    - `authn/` - Authentication storage (Redis, memory, static)\n    - `environments/` - Environment-aware storage with Git support\n    - `fs/` - File system abstraction layer\n    - `git/` - Git repository management\n  - `telemetry/` - Usage telemetry collection\n- `rpc/` - Protocol buffer definitions and generated code\n  - `flipt/` - Main Flipt API (v1)\n  - `v2/` - V2 APIs (environments, analytics, evaluation)\n- `sdk/` - Client SDKs (Go SDK included)\n- `ui/` - React/TypeScript frontend\n\n### Key Components\n\n#### Storage Layer\n\nMulti-backend storage supporting:\n\n- Git repositories (primary for v2)\n- File system (local development)\n- Cloud storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) - Flipt config only\n\n#### Environments System\n\n- Environment-per-branch: Map Git branches to environments\n- Environment-per-directory: Organize by microservice/team\n- Environment-per-repository: Separate repos for products\n\n#### APIs\n\n- gRPC API (port 9000) - Primary interface\n- REST API (port 8080) - HTTP gateway over gRPC\n- OpenFeature compatible evaluation\n\n##### Key Endpoints\n\n- **Evaluation API**: `/api/v1/evaluate` - Evaluate feature flags\n- **Management API**: Full CRUD operations on flags, segments, rules\n- **Analytics API**: `/api/v2/analytics` - Flag evaluation metrics\n- **Environments API**: `/api/v2/environments` - Environment management\n- **OFREP API**: `/ofrep/v1` - OpenFeature Remote Evaluation Protocol\n\n#### UI Architecture\n\n- React 19 with TypeScript\n- Redux Toolkit for state management\n- Tailwind CSS for styling\n- Vite for build tooling\n- React Router for navigation\n\n## Development Setup & Commands\n\n### Initial Setup\n\n```bash\n# Install required development tools\nmage bootstrap\n```\n\n### Development Workflow\n\n1. **Setup**: Run `mage bootstrap` to install tools\n2. **Backend Development**:\n   - Use `mage go:run` for server\n   - Server runs on port 8080 (HTTP) and 9000 (gRPC)\n3. **Frontend Development**:\n   - Run `mage ui:run` for UI dev server on port 5173\n   - UI proxies API requests to backend on port 8080\n4. **Full Development**: Run both servers simultaneously\n\n### Build & Run Commands\n\n- `mage build` - Builds the project similar to a release build (default target)\n- `mage go:build` - Builds Go server for development without bundling assets\n- `mage go:run` - Runs Go server in development mode with local config\n- `mage dev` - Alias for go:run\n- `./bin/flipt server --config config/local.yml` - Run built binary with local config\n\n### UI Development Commands\n\n- `mage ui:deps` - Install UI dependencies\n- `mage ui:run` - Run UI in development mode (port 5173)\n- `mage ui:dev` - Alias for ui:run\n- `mage ui:build` - Build UI assets for release\n- `cd ui && npm run dev` - Alternative way to run UI dev server\n\n### Testing Commands\n\n- `mage go:test` - Run all Go unit tests\n- `go test -v {path} -run {test}` - Run a specific Go test\n- `mage go:bench` - Run Go benchmarking tests\n- `mage go:cover` - Run tests and generate coverage report\n- `cd ui && npm run test` - Run UI unit tests (Jest)\n\n### Code Quality Commands\n\n- `mage go:lint` - Run Go linters (golangci-lint and buf lint)\n- `mage go:fmt` - Format Go code with goimports\n- `mage ui:lint` - Run UI linters (ESLint)\n- `mage ui:fmt` - Format UI code (Prettier)\n\n### Code Generation Commands\n\n- `mage go:proto` - Generate protobuf files and gRPC stubs\n- `mage go:mockery` - Generate mocks\n- `mage go:generate` - Generate both mocks and proto files\n\n### Cleanup Commands\n\n- `mage clean` - Clean built files and tidy go.mod\n- `mage prep` - Prepare project for building (clean + ui:build)\n\n### Build Process Notes\n\n- Protocol buffers generate Go and gateway code\n- Mockery generates Go test mocks. See .mockery.yml\n- UI has no code generation\n\n## Code Style Guidelines\n\n### Go Code Style\n\n#### Foundation: Google Go Style Guide\n\n**All Go code should follow the [Google Go Style Guide](https://google.github.io/styleguide/go/guide) as the base style guide.**\n\nThe guidelines below are Flipt-specific conventions that build upon the Google style guide. When in doubt, refer to the Google guide first, then apply these project-specific patterns.\n\nKey areas from the Google guide to pay special attention to:\n\n- **Formatting**: Use `gofmt` (we use `goimports` which includes `gofmt`)\n- **Naming**: Follow Go naming conventions for packages, types, functions, and variables\n- **Package organization**: Keep packages focused and avoid circular dependencies\n- **Error handling**: Return errors as the last return value, handle errors explicitly\n- **Documentation**: Write clear godoc comments for exported types and functions\n\n#### Naming Conventions\n\n- **Public functions**: Use PascalCase (`NewServer`, `ListFlags`, `RegisterGRPC`)\n- **Private functions**: Use camelCase (`getStore`, `buildConfig`, `handleError`)\n- **Factory functions**: Use `New` prefix for constructors (`NewRepository`, `NewEnvironmentFactory`)\n- **Variables**: Use camelCase (`environmentKey`, `namespaceKey`, `startTime`)\n- **Constants**: Use PascalCase for exported, camelCase for unexported\n- **Interfaces**: Use descriptive names (`Store`, `EnvironmentStore`, `Validator`)\n\n#### Variable Declarations\n\n```go\n// Prefer := for single variable declarations\nresult := SomeType{}\n\n// Use var blocks for multiple related variables\nvar (\n    environmentKey = r.EnvironmentKey\n    namespaceKey   = r.NamespaceKey\n    startTime      = time.Now().UTC()\n)\n```\n\n#### Constants\n\nUse constants when appropriate for values that are unlikely to change. Even in tests.\n\n**❌ Bad Examples**\n\n```go\nvar (\n    maxRetries = 3\n    secretProvider = \"vault\"\n)\n```\n\n```go\n  maxRetries := 3\n  secretProvider := \"vault\"\n```\n\n**✅ Good Example**\n\n```go\nconst (\n    maxRetries = 3\n    secretProvider = \"vault\"\n)\n```\n\n#### Error Handling\n\n##### Error Types\n\nUse the custom error types when possible:\n\n```go\n// Preferred - use custom error types\nreturn nil, errs.ErrNotFoundf(\"flag %q not found\", key)\nreturn nil, errs.ErrInvalidf(\"invalid configuration: %s\", reason)\n\n// Fallback - standard error handling\nreturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to process: %w\", err)\n```\n\n##### Inline Errors\n\nPrefer inline error declarations and checking when it makes sense.\n\n**❌ Bad Example**\n\n```go\nerr := doSomething()\nif err != nil {\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n**✅ Good Example**\n\n```go\nif err := doSomething(); err != nil {\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\n#### Imports\n\nFollow the three-group import pattern (extends Google guide):\n\n```go\nimport (\n    // Standard library\n    \"context\"\n    \"fmt\"\n    \"time\"\n\n    // Third-party packages\n    \"github.com/go-git/go-git/v6\"\n    \"go.uber.org/zap\"\n\n    // Local packages\n    \"go.flipt.io/flipt/errors\"\n    \"go.flipt.io/flipt/internal/config\"\n)\n```\n\n#### Logging\n\nUse structured logging with zap:\n\n```go\n// Preferred - structured logging with context\ns.logger.Debug(\"processing request\", \n    zap.String(\"environment\", envKey),\n    zap.String(\"namespace\", nsKey),\n    zap.Int(\"count\", len(items)))\n\n// Use appropriate log levels\ns.logger.Debug(\"debug info for development\")  // Most common\ns.logger.Info(\"important application events\")\ns.logger.Warn(\"recoverable error conditions\")\ns.logger.Error(\"error conditions that need attention\")\n\n// Avoid overusing log statements - be selective about what to log\n```\n\n#### Comments and Documentation\n\n```go\n// Public functions need godoc comments starting with function name\n// ListFlags lists all flags in the specified environment and namespace\nfunc (s *Server) ListFlags(ctx context.Context, r *flipt.ListFlagRequest) (*flipt.FlagList, error) {\n    // Inline comments explain complex logic\n    // Check for X-Environment header for backward compatibility\n    environmentKey := r.EnvironmentKey\n    if headerEnv, ok := common.FliptEnvironmentFromContext(ctx); ok && headerEnv != \"\" {\n        environmentKey = headerEnv\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### Project Organization\n\n- Use `internal/` for private packages that shouldn't be imported externally\n- Organize by domain (`server/`, `storage/`, `config/`, `analytics/`)\n- Keep interfaces in domain root, implementations in subdirectories\n- Co-locate tests with implementation (`*_test.go`)\n- Use `testdata/` directories for test fixtures\n\n#### Pre-commit Quality Checks (Go)\n\n**Always run these commands when adding or editing Go code:**\n\n```bash\n# Format Go code\nmage go:fmt\n\n# Lint Go code\nmage go:lint\n\n# Run modernize to update code to 1.24+ style\nmage go:modernize\n```\n\n### UI/React/TypeScript Code Style\n\n#### File Naming and Organization\n\n- **Components**: Use PascalCase for `.tsx` files (`FlagForm.tsx`, `FlagTable.tsx`)\n- **API files**: Use camelCase with suffix (`flagsApi.ts`, `authApi.ts`)\n- **Types**: Use PascalCase for `.ts` files (`Flag.ts`, `Analytics.ts`)\n- **Utilities**: Use descriptive lowercase names (`helpers.ts`)\n\n#### Component Structure\n\n- Use functional components with hooks\n- Organize components by domain in directories\n- Keep reusable UI components in `ui/` directory\n- Co-locate component tests with implementation\n\n#### State Management\n\n- Use Redux Toolkit for application state\n- Create API slices for domain-specific data (`flagsApi.ts`)\n- Use TypeScript for type safety throughout\n\n#### Pre-commit Quality Checks (UI)\n\n**Always run these commands when adding or editing UI code:**\n\n```bash\n# Format UI code (TypeScript/React)\nmage ui:fmt\n\n# Lint UI code (ESLint)\nmage ui:lint\n```\n\n### General Pre-commit Checks\n\n```bash\n# Format specific markdown files (replace with actual filename)\nnpx prettier -w README.md\n```\n\n## Configuration\n\n### Configuration Files\n\n- `config/local.yml` - Local development config\n- `config/dev.yml` - Alternative dev config\n- User config at `{{ USER_CONFIG_DIR }}/flipt/config.yml`\n\n### Debugging Tips\n\n- Use `FLIPT_LOG_LEVEL=debug` for verbose logging\n- Check `/metrics` endpoint for Prometheus metrics\n- View OpenTelemetry traces for request flow\n- Git sync issues: Check repository access and credentials\n- Authentication issues: Verify redirect URLs and client configurations\n\n## Testing Guidelines\n\n### Test Structure\n\n- Go tests use testcontainers for unit tests that depend on external tools\n- UI tests use Jest for unit tests and Playwright for E2E\n\n### Writing Effective Tests\n\nWhen writing tests, ensure they actually verify the functionality being tested:\n\n**❌ Bad Example - Test doesn't verify actual behavior:**\n\n```go\n// This test only verifies the method runs without error, not that the logic works\nfunc TestFeature(t *testing.T) {\n    result, err := service.DoSomething(input)\n    require.NoError(t, err)\n    assert.NotNil(t, result)\n}\n```\n\n**✅ Good Example - Test verifies specific behavior:**\n\n```go\n// This test verifies the actual logic and behavior\nfunc TestFeature(t *testing.T) {\n    // Use mock.MatchedBy to verify the correct data is passed through\n    store.On(\"Method\", mock.MatchedBy(func(ctx context.Context) bool {\n        // Verify the context contains the expected values\n        value, ok := somePackage.ValueFromContext(ctx)\n        return ok && value == expectedValue\n    }), expectedArg).Return(expectedResult, nil)\n    \n    result, err := service.DoSomething(input)\n    require.NoError(t, err)\n    \n    // Verify the actual business logic worked correctly\n    assert.Equal(t, expectedOutput, result.ImportantField)\n    store.AssertExpectations(t) // Ensures mocks were called as expected\n}\n```\n\n**Key Testing Principles:**\n\n- **Verify behavior, not just success**: Don't just check that methods return without error\n- **Test the logic**: Use `mock.MatchedBy` to verify correct data flows through the system\n- **Test edge cases**: Include tests for when conditions are met vs. not met\n- **Use meaningful assertions**: Assert on the specific values that matter to the business logic\n- **Mock verification**: Use `mock.AssertExpectations(t)` to ensure mocks were called with expected parameters\n\n### Integration Tests\n\nIntegration tests are defined in `build/testing/` and run as Mage targets. For details on available integration test targets, helpers, and how to add new integration tests, see the files in that directory:\n\n- `build/testing/integration.go` - Integration test Mage targets and configuration\n- `build/testing/integration/` - Individual integration test suites\n- `build/testing/helpers.go` - Shared test helpers\n- `build/testing/test.go` - Core test utilities\n\n## Git Workflow & Commits\n\n### Creating Effective Commits\n\n#### Commit Message Format\n\nFollow conventional commit format:\n\n```\ntype(scope): brief description\n\nLonger description if needed explaining the why, not the what.\n\n- List specific changes if helpful\n- Include any breaking changes\n- Reference issues (e.g., \"Fixes #123\")\n```\n\n#### Commit Types\n\n- `feat:` - New features\n- `fix:` - Bug fixes\n- `docs:` - Documentation changes\n- `style:` - Code style changes (formatting, etc.)\n- `refactor:` - Code refactoring without functional changes\n- `test:` - Adding or modifying tests\n- `chore:` - Build process or auxiliary tool changes\n\n#### Examples\n\n```bash\n# Good commit messages\ngit commit -s -m \"feat: add X-Environment header support to ListFlags endpoint\n\nThe v1 ListFlags endpoint now checks for X-Environment header \nfor backward compatibility. When present, the header value \ntakes precedence over the request environment parameter.\n\n- Modified ListFlags method to check context for environment header\n- Added ForwardFliptEnvironment middleware to v1 API gateway\n- Added comprehensive test coverage\n\nFixes #4411\"\n\ngit commit -s -m \"fix: resolve race condition in flag evaluation cache\"\n\ngit commit -s -m \"test: improve X-Environment header test coverage\n\n- Use mock.MatchedBy to verify context contains correct environment\n- Add test for when header is not present\n- Ensure tests actually validate the header precedence logic\"\n```\n\n### Pull Request Guidelines\n\n#### PR Title Format\n\nUse the same conventional commit format for PR titles:\n\n```\ntype(scope): brief description\n```\n\n#### PR Description Template\n\n```markdown\n## Summary\n\nBrief description of what this PR does and why.\n\n## Changes\n\n- **Modified `file1.go`**: Specific change description\n- **Added `file2_test.go`**: Test coverage description\n- **Updated `config.yml`**: Configuration change description\n\n## Backward Compatibility\n\nDescribe any backward compatibility or breaking change considerations.\n\n## Additional Notes\n\nAny other context, screenshots, or related issues.\n```\n\n#### PR Best Practices\n\n- **Keep PRs focused**: One logical change per PR\n- **Write descriptive titles**: Use conventional commit format\n- **Provide context**: Explain the \"why\" behind changes\n- **Include tests**: Always add tests for new functionality\n- **Run quality checks**: Format and lint before creating PR\n- **Reference issues**: Use \"Fixes #123\" to auto-close issues\n- **Review your own PR**: Look through the diff before requesting review\n- **Base PRs on the correct branch**: Base PRs on the correct branch (e.g. `v2` for v2 changes, `main` for v1 changes)\n- **Label PRs correctly**: When creating a feature based off of the v2 branch, label the PR with the `v2` label. Similarly for a v1 (main) feature, use the `v1` label.\n\n#### Quality Checklist\n\nBefore creating a PR, ensure:\n\n- [ ] Code follows style guidelines\n- [ ] Tests are added and passing\n- [ ] Linting passes (`mage go:lint` / `mage ui:lint`)\n- [ ] Code is formatted (`mage go:fmt` / `mage ui:fmt`)\n- [ ] Commit messages are clear and descriptive\n- [ ] PR description explains the change and its purpose\n","numLines":571,"startLine":1,"totalLines":571}}}

len(s.shutdownFuncs) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n   454→\t\tif fn := s.shutdownFuncs[i]; fn != nil {\n   455→\t\t\tif err := fn(ctx); err != nil {\n   456→\t\t\t\treturn err\n   457→\t\t\t}\n   458→\t\t}\n *zap.Logger\n\tmigrator *migrate.Migrate\n}\n\nfunc migratorHelper(logger *zap.Logger, db *sql.DB, driver Driver, databaseDriver database.Driver) (*Migrator, error) {\n\t// source migrations from {\n\t\tif !errors.Is(err, migrate.ErrNilVersion) {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"getting current migrations version: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tm.logger.Debug(\"first run, running dependencies.\nfunc (s *GRPCServer) Shutdown(ctx context.Context) error {\n\ts.logger.Info(\"shutting down GRPC server...\")\n\n\t// call in reverse order to emulate pop semantics of a stack\n\tfor i := len(s.shutdownFuncs) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n\t\tif fn := s.shutdownFuncs[i]; fn != nil {\n\t\t\tif err := fn(ctx); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\ntype Fix any issues found.\n\n## Phase 1: Identify Changes\n\nRun `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.\n\n## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel\n\nUse the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.\n\n### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review\n\nFor each change:\n\n1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Use Grep to find similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.\n2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.\n3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.\n\n### Agent 2: Code Quality Review\n\nReview the same changes for hacky patterns:\n\n1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls\n2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones\n3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction\n4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries\n5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase\n\n### Agent 3: Efficiency Review\n\nReview the same changes for efficiency:\n\n1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns\n2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel\n3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths\n4. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error\n5. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks\n6. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one\n\n## Phase 3: Fix Issues\n\nWait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. If a finding is a false positive or not worth addressing, note it and move on — do not argue with the finding, just skip it.\n\nWhen done, briefly summarize what was fixed (or confirm the code was already clean).\n\n\n## Additional Focus\n\n/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"40fdedad-516e-44ae-8e79-b5204419db79","uuid":"4ff8e1cf-573d-479f-8c39-406014fdf2a5","isSynthetic":true}

72→\t\treq.EnvironmentKey,\n    73→\t\treq.NamespaceKey,\n    74→\t\treq.FlagKey,\n    75→\t\treq.StepMinutes,\n    76→\t)\n    77→\tr := promapi.Range{\n    78→\t\tStart: req.From.UTC(),\n    79→\t\tEnd:   req.To.Add(time.Duration(req.StepMinutes) * time.Minute).UTC(),\n    80→\t\tStep:  time.Duration(req.StepMinutes) * time.Minute,\n    81→\t}\n    82→\tdata, warnings, err := c.promClient.QueryRange(ctx, query, r)\n    83→\tif err != nil {\n    84→\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n    85→\t}\n    86→\n    87→\tif len(warnings) > 0 {\n    88→\t\tc.logger.Warn(\"prometheus query returned warnings\", zap.Strings(\"warnings\", warnings))\n    89→\t}\n    90→\n    91→\tm, ok := data.(model.Matrix)\n    92→\tif !ok || len(m) == 0 {\n    93→\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unexpected data type returned from prometheus\")\n    94→\t}\n    95→\n    96→\tv := m[len(m)-1]\n    97→\tvar (\n    98→\t\ttimestamps = make([]string, len(v.Values))\n    99→\t\tvalues     = make([]float32, len(v.Values))\n   100→\t)\n   101→\n   102→\tfor i, vv := range v.Values {\n   103→\t\ttimestamps[i] = time.UnixMilli(int64(vv.Timestamp)).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)\n   104→\t\tvalues[i] = float32(math.Round(float64(vv.Value)))\n   105→\t}\n   106→\n   107→\t// Prometheus returns one data series for our query with vector(0).\n   108→\t// Victoria Metrics returns one or two data series: one for the actual data and one for\n   109→\t// the vector(0). The actual data has only points with non-zero values or may absent at all\n   110→\t// if there is no data. The vector(0) has only points with zero values. We need to combine it\n   111→\tfor i := len(m) - 2; i >= 0; i-- {\n   112→\t\tfor _, vv := range m[i].Values {\n   113→\t\t\tt := time.UnixMilli(int64(vv.Timestamp)).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)\n   114→\t\t\tif i := slices.Index(timestamps, t); i != -1 {\n   115→\t\t\t\tvalues[i] += float32(math.Round(float64(vv.Value)))\n   116→\t\t\t}\n   117→\t\t}\n   118→\t}\n   119→\n   120→\treturn timestamps, values, nil\n   121→}\n   122→\n   123→// GetBatchFlagEvaluationsCount retrieves evaluation counts for multiple flags in a single query\n   124→func (c *client) with empty flag key (from vector(0))\n   160→\tfor _, series := range m {\n   161→\t\tflagKey := string(series.Metric[\"flipt_flag\"])\n   162→\t\tif flagKey == \"\" {\n   163→\t\t\tbaseTimestamps = 167→\t\t\tbreak\n   168→\t\t}\n   169→\t}\n   170→\n   171→\t// If we didn't find a base series, use timestamps from the first series\n   172→\tif len(baseTimestamps) == 0 && len(m) > 0 {\n   173→\t\tseries := m[0]\n   174→\t\tbaseTimestamps = make([]string, len(series.Values))\n   175→\t\tfor i, v := range series.Values {\n   176→\t\t\tbaseTimestamps[i] = time.UnixMilli(int64(v.Timestamp)).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)\n   177→\t\t}\n   178→\t}\n   179→\n   180→\t// Map to store results by flag key\n   181→\tresult := 195→\t\tflagKey := string(series.Metric[\"flipt_flag\"])\n   196→\n   197→\t\t// Skip series with empty flag keys (from vector(0))\n   198→\t\tif flagKey == \"\" {\n   199→\t\t\tcontinue\n   200→\t\t}\n   201→\n   202→\t\t// Skip series for flags we didn't request\n   203→\t\tif !slices.Contains(req.FlagKeys, flagKey) {\n   204→\t\t\tcontinue\n   205→\t\t}\n   206→\n   207→\t\t// Create a result using the consistent timeline\n   215→\t\tflagData := result[flagKey]\n   216→\t\tfor i, ts := range flagData.Timestamps {\n   217→\t\t\tif value, exists := timestampToValue[ts]; exists {\n   218→\t\t\t\tflagData.Values[i] = value\n   219→\t\t\t}\n   220→\t\t\t// If the timestamp doesn't exist in this series, it keeps the zero value\n   221→\t\t}\n   222→\n   223→\t\tresult[flagKey] = flagData\n   224→\t}\n   225→\n   226→\t// Apply limit if specified\n   227→\tif req.Limit > 0 && len(baseTimestamps) > req.Limit {\n   228→\t\tfor flagKey, data := range result {\n   229→\t\t\tfactor := len(data.Timestamps) / req.Limit\n   230→\t\t\tcompressedValues := make([]float32, req.Limit)\n   231→\t\t\tcompressedTimestamps := make([]string, req.Limit)\n   232→\n   233→\t\t\tfor i := range req.Limit {\n   234→\t\t\t\tstart := i * factor\n   235→\t\t\t\tend := min((i+1)*factor, len(data.Values))\n   236→\n   237→\t\t\t\tsum := float32(0)\n   238→\t\t\t\tfor j := start; j < end; j++ {\n   239→\t\t\t\t\tsum += data.Values[j]\n   240→\t\t\t\t}\n   241→\n   242→\t\t\t\t// Avoid division by zero\n   243→\t\t\t\tif end > start {\n   244→\t\t\t\t\tcompressedValues[i] = sum / float32(end-start)\n   245→\t\t\t\t}\n   246→\t\t\t\tcompressedTimestamps[i] = data.Timestamps[start]\n   247→\t\t\t}\n   248→\n   249→\t\t\tresult[flagKey] = panalytics.FlagEvaluationData{\n   250→\t\t\t\tTimestamps: compressedTimestamps,\n   251→\t\t\t\tValues:     compressedValues,\n   252→\t\t\t}\n   253→\t\t}\n   254→\t}\n   255→\n   256→\treturn result, nil\n   257→}\n   258→\n Now I have all the information I need to write a comprehensive efficiency review. Let me create the report:\n\n## Code Efficiency Review: Flipt CockroachDB Analytics Backend\n\nI've completed a thorough analysis of the CockroachDB analytics backend implementation. Below are my efficiency findings organized by category and severity.\n\n---\n\n## 1. CRITICAL EFFICIENCY ISSUES\n\n### Issue 1.1: Missing Connection Pool Configuration\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (lines 66-77)\n\n**Problem**: \nThe `connect()` function uses `sql.Open()` without configuring connection pool parameters:\n```go\nfunc connect(cfg config.CockroachDBConfig) (*sql.DB, error) {\n\tconn, err := sql.Open(\"postgres\", cfg.URL)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tif err := conn.Ping(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn conn, nil\n}\n```\n\nThe default connection pool settings in Go's `database/sql` are:\n- `MaxOpenConns`: unlimited (can cause resource exhaustion)\n- `MaxIdleConns`: 2 (too low for analytics workloads)\n- `ConnMaxLifetime`: unlimited (connections not recycled)\n\n**Impact**: **HIGH** - For analytics workloads with multiple concurrent queries, this will:\n- Create unbounded connections under load\n- Exhaust database connection limits\n- Cause connection pool starvation\n- Lead to cascading failures\n\n**Recommendation**:\n```go\nfunc connect(cfg config.CockroachDBConfig) (*sql.DB, error) {\n\tconn, err := sql.Open(\"postgres\", cfg.URL)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Configure connection pool for analytics workloads\n\tconn.SetMaxOpenConns(25)      // Reasonable limit for analytics queries\n\tconn.SetMaxIdleConns(5)       // Keep some connections ready\n\tconn.SetConnMaxLifetime(5 * time.Minute)  // Recycle long-lived connections\n\n\tif err := conn.Ping(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn conn, nil\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Issue 1.2: Unbounded Slice Allocations in Hot Path\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (lines 117-135 and 180-204)\n\n**Problem**:\nIn `GetFlagEvaluationsCount()` and `GetBatchFlagEvaluationsCount()`, slices are initialized with zero capacity and grown via `append()`:\n\n```go\nvar (\n    timestamps = make([]string, 0)  // Zero capacity - inefficient for growth\n    values     = make([]float32, 0)\n)\n\nfor rows.Next() {\n    // ... \n    timestamps.append(timestamps, timestamp)  // Causes repeated allocations\n    values.append(values, float32(value))\n}\n```\n\nEach `append()` that exceeds capacity triggers a reallocation (typically doubling capacity). For large datasets (millions of analytics records), this causes:\n- Repeated memory allocations\n- Garbage collection pressure\n- Cache misses\n\n**Impact**: **MEDIUM-HIGH** - Noticeable performance degradation with large result sets.\n\n**Recommendation**:\nPre-allocate with capacity hints from the query or use an estimated upper bound:\n\n```go\n// Option 1: If the row count is known or can be estimated\ntimestamps := make([]string, 0, estimatedCapacity)\nvalues := make([]float32, 0, estimatedCapacity)\n\n// Option 2: Common pattern - allocate with a reasonable initial size\ntimestamps := make([]string, 0, 100)  // Common case: 100 data points\nvalues := make([]float32, 0, 100)\n```\n\nAlternatively, use `append` once before the loop if you can afford to read all rows:\n```go\n// Collect into temp slice first, then allocate exact size\nvar tempTimestamps []string\nvar tempValues []float32\nfor rows.Next() {\n    // append to temp slices\n}\ntimestamps := tempTimestamps  // Now allocation is done once\nvalues := tempValues\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. QUERY EFFICIENCY ISSUES\n\n### Issue 2.1: Inefficient Time Bucketing Calculation\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (lines 88-91, 148)\n\n**Problem**:\nThe time bucketing calculation is inefficient:\n```sql\nEXTRACT(EPOCH FROM DATE_TRUNC('minute', timestamp))::bigint / ($4 * 60) * ($4 * 60) AS timestamp_bucket\n```\n\nThis query:\n1. Converts timestamp to EPOCH (seconds since 1970)\n2. Divides by `StepMinutes * 60` (the computation happens at runtime for EVERY row)\n3. Multiplies back by `StepMinutes * 60`\n\nThe CPU-intensive arithmetic is performed for every row in the result set. For millions of rows, this is wasteful.\n\n**Additional Problem** (line 148):\nAfter getting the bucketed timestamp from the database, the application recalculates it again:\n```go\ntimestamp := time.Unix(timestampBucket, 0).UTC().Format(time.DateTime)\n```\n\nThis is redundant work: the database already computed the bucket, and then the application converts it back to a formatted string, doing extra work.\n\n**Impact**: **MEDIUM** - Scales poorly with dataset size. The calculation is repeated millions of times.\n\n**Recommendation**:\nLet the database format the timestamp more efficiently:\n\n```sql\n-- Instead of arithmetic, use DATE_TRUNC directly with the interval\nSELECT\n    DATE_TRUNC('minute', timestamp) AS timestamp_bucket,\n    SUM(value) AS value\nFROM flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2\nWHERE environment_key = $1 \n  AND namespace_key = $2 \n  AND flag_key = $3 \n  AND timestamp >= $4 \n  AND timestamp < $5\nGROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('minute', timestamp)\nORDER BY timestamp_bucket ASC\n```\n\nIf variable step bucketing is needed, use:\n```sql\n-- For 5-minute buckets, for example\nDATE_TRUNC('minute', timestamp + interval '2 minutes 30 seconds')\n  AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' - interval '2 minutes 30 seconds'\n```\n\nOr pre-calculate the bucket width in the application and pass it as a parameter (less CPU in DB).\n\n---\n\n### Issue 2.2: Missing LIMIT Enforcement in Single-Flag Query\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (lines 88-101)\n\n**Problem**:\n`GetFlagEvaluationsCount()` has no LIMIT clause. If a user requests analytics for a very wide time window (e.g., 1 year), the query will fetch potentially millions of rows into memory:\n\n```sql\nSELECT\n    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM DATE_TRUNC('minute', timestamp))::bigint / ($4 * 60) * ($4 * 60) AS timestamp_bucket,\n    SUM(value) AS value\nFROM flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2\nWHERE environment_key = $1 AND namespace_key = $2 AND flag_key = $3 AND ...\nGROUP BY timestamp_bucket\nORDER BY timestamp_bucket ASC\n-- NO LIMIT!\n```\n\n**Impact**: **MEDIUM** - Unbounded memory usage on large time windows.\n\n**Recommendation**:\nAdd a reasonable LIMIT (e.g., 1440 data points = 1 day of 1-minute data). For requests exceeding this, the client can implement pagination or request larger time steps:\n\n```go\nconst maxDataPoints = 1440  // 1 day of minute-granularity data\n\nrows, err := c.Conn.QueryContext(ctx, `\n    SELECT ... \n    GROUP BY timestamp_bucket\n    ORDER BY timestamp_bucket ASC\n    LIMIT $6\n`,\n    req.EnvironmentKey,\n    req.NamespaceKey,\n    req.FlagKey,\n    req.StepMinutes,\n    req.From,\n    req.To,\n    maxDataPoints,\n)\n```\n\n---\n\n### Issue 2.3: Suboptimal Batch Query with ANY() and LIMIT\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (lines 154, 159)\n\n**Problem**:\nThe batch query uses `flag_key = ANY($7)` which may not use indexes efficiently in all cases:\n\n```sql\nWHERE\n    environment_key = $1 AND\n    namespace_key = $2 AND\n    flag_key = ANY($7) AND  -- May have suboptimal index plan\n    timestamp >= $5 AND\n    timestamp < $6\n```\n\nAdditionally, `LIMIT $8` is applied AFTER the GROUP BY and ORDER BY, which means:\n1. All matching rows are grouped and aggregated\n2. All results are sorted\n3. Only then is the LIMIT applied\n\nFor 1000 flags with months of data, this aggregates massive amounts of data before discarding most of it.\n\n**Impact**: **MEDIUM** - Query planner may choose suboptimal execution paths; unnecessary aggregation work.\n\n**Recommendation**:\n1. Create a composite index on `(environment_key, namespace_key, flag_key, timestamp)` in migrations\n2. Push the limit down earlier in the query:\n\n```sql\n-- Better: Use DISTINCT ON or window functions to get top N per flag\nWITH ranked_data AS (\n    SELECT\n        flag_key,\n        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM DATE_TRUNC('minute', timestamp))::bigint / ($4 * 60) * ($4 * 60) AS timestamp_bucket,\n        SUM(value) AS value,\n        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY flag_key ORDER BY timestamp_bucket DESC) AS rn\n    FROM flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2\n    WHERE environment_key = $1 \n      AND namespace_key = $2 \n      AND flag_key = ANY($7) \n      AND timestamp >= $5 \n      AND timestamp < $6\n    GROUP BY flag_key, timestamp_bucket\n)\nSELECT flag_key, timestamp_bucket, value\nFROM ranked_data\nWHERE rn <= $8  -- Limit per flag, not total\nORDER BY flag_key, timestamp_bucket ASC\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. MEMORY AND DATA HANDLING ISSUES\n\n### Issue 3.1: Inefficient Batch Query Result Processing\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (lines 141-204)\n\n**Problem**:\nIn `GetBatchFlagEvaluationsCount()`, the code creates intermediate objects for every row:\n\n```go\ndata := result[flagKey]\ndata.Timestamps = append(data.Timestamps, timestamp)\ndata.Values = append(data.Values, float32(value))\nresult[flagKey] = data  // Unnecessary copy back to map\n```\n\nThis pattern:\n1. Retrieves the struct from the map (copy)\n2. Modifies the copy\n3. Writes it back to the map (another copy)\n\nThis is inefficient for large result sets. Each row incurs unnecessary memory copies.\n\n**Impact**: **LOW-MEDIUM** - Constant factor overhead on memory operations.\n\n**Recommendation**:\nStore pointers in the map or use direct field access:\n\n```go\n// Option 1: Use pointers\nresult := make(map[string]*analytics.FlagEvaluationData)\n\nfor rows.Next() {\n    // ...\n    if _, ok := result[flagKey]; !ok {\n        result[flagKey] = &analytics.FlagEvaluationData{\n            Timestamps: make([]string, 0),\n            Values:     make([]float32, 0),\n        }\n    }\n    \n    result[flagKey].Timestamps = append(result[flagKey].Timestamps, timestamp)\n    result[flagKey].Values = append(result[flagKey].Values, float32(value))\n}\n\n// Then convert to non-pointer map before returning\nflagData := make(map[string]analytics.FlagEvaluationData)\nfor key, data := range result {\n    flagData[key] = *data\n}\nreturn flagData, nil\n```\n\n---\n\n### Issue 3.2: Unnecessary Ping() Call in Startup Path\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (line 72) and `/workspace/flipt/internal/storage/analytics/db.go` (line 34)\n\n**Problem**:\nThere are TWO separate `Ping()` calls in sequence:\n1. In `db.go` `open()` function (line 34)\n2. In `client.go` `connect()` function (line 72)\n\nBoth execute during the one-time initialization in `dbOnce.Do()`:\n\n```go\n// In db.go\nif err := db.Ping(); err != nil {\n    return nil, \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to ping CockroachDB: %w\", err)\n}\n```\n\n```go\n// In client.go \nif err := conn.Ping(); err != nil {\n    return nil, err\n}\n```\n\n`Ping()` adds latency to startup. With two pings, startup is unnecessarily slow.\n\n**Impact**: **LOW** - Affects startup time only (not hot path), but unnecessary.\n\n**Recommendation**:\nRemove one redundant Ping call. If checking connectivity is important, do it once in `connect()`:\n\n```go\n// In client.go - this is sufficient\nfunc connect(cfg config.CockroachDBConfig) (*sql.DB, error) {\n    conn, err := sql.Open(\"postgres\", cfg.URL)\n    if err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n\n    // Only ping here\n    if err := conn.Ping(); err != nil {\n        return nil, err\n    }\n\n    return conn, nil\n}\n\n// Remove the second ping from db.go\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. INITIALIZATION AND STARTUP ISSUES\n\n### Issue 4.1: Blocking Migration in Synchronous Initialization\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (lines 29-43)\n\n**Problem**:\nThe `New()` constructor uses `sync.Once.Do()` to run migrations synchronously:\n\n```go\nvar dbOnce sync.Once\n\nfunc New(logger *zap.Logger, cfg *config.Config, forceMigrate bool) (*Client, error) {\n    var conn *sql.DB\n    var cockroachErr error\n\n    dbOnce.Do(func() {\n        err := runMigrations(logger, cfg, forceMigrate)  // Blocks startup\n        if err != nil {\n            cockroachErr = err\n            return\n        }\n        // ... connection establishment\n    })\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\nIf this is called from the gRPC server startup (`grpc.go`), it blocks the entire server from starting. For a server with large databases, migrations can take seconds or minutes.\n\n**Impact**: **MEDIUM** - Startup latency adds to deployment time; if migrations fail, the entire server fails to start.\n\n**Recommendation**:\n1. Run migrations separately from server startup (already available via `flipt migrate` CLI)\n2. Or, check if migrations are up-to-date but don't force them:\n\n```go\n// In migrator.go - only check version, don't auto-migrate\nfunc (m *Migrator) UpIfNeeded() error {\n    currentVersion, _, err := m.migrator.Version()\n    if err != nil {\n        if !errors.Is(err, migrate.ErrNilVersion) {\n            return fmt.Errorf(\"checking migrations: %w\", err)\n        }\n        // First run - fail with helpful message\n        return errors.New(\"no migrations found - please run 'flipt migrate' first\")\n    }\n\n    expectedVersion := expectedVersions[m.driver]\n    if currentVersion < expectedVersion {\n        return fmt.Errorf(\"migrations out of date: current=%d, expected=%d - please run 'flipt migrate'\", \n            currentVersion, expectedVersion)\n    }\n    return nil\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Issue 4.2: sync.Once Usage with Error Handling Anti-pattern\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/server/analytics/cockroachdb/client.go` (lines 16, 29-47)\n\n**Problem**:\nUsing `sync.Once` with error handling is awkward:\n\n```go\nvar dbOnce sync.Once\n\nfunc New(logger *zap.Logger, cfg *config.Config, forceMigrate bool) (*Client, error) {\n    var (\n        conn          *sql.DB\n        cockroachErr  error  // Must declare outside to capture error\n    )\n\n    dbOnce.Do(func() {\n        // ... error handling inside closure\n        cockroachErr = err\n        return\n    })\n\n    if cockroachErr != nil {\n        return nil, cockroachErr\n    }\n    // ...\n}\n```\n\nThis pattern is fragile:\n- If `cockroachErr` is not initialized before the first call fails, the error is lost\n- The error type is awkward (interface{} -> error)\n- Similar pattern used in ClickHouse client as well (code duplication)\n\n**Impact**: **LOW** - Maintainability issue; potential for hidden bugs if error handling is refactored.\n\n**Recommendation**:\nUse a cleaner pattern with `sync.Once` + pointer to result:\n\n```go\nvar (\n    dbOnce   sync.Once\n    dbResult *dbInitResult\n)\n\ntype dbInitResult struct {\n    conn  *sql.DB\n    err   error\n}\n\nfunc New(logger *zap.Logger, cfg *config.Config, forceMigrate bool) (*Client, error) {\n    dbOnce.Do(func() {\n        conn, err := initDB(logger, cfg, forceMigrate)\n        dbResult = &dbInitResult{conn: conn, err: err}\n    })\n\n    if dbResult.err != nil {\n        return nil, dbResult.err\n    }\n\n    return &Client{Conn: dbResult.conn}, nil\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. INDEX COVERAGE ISSUES\n\n### Issue 5.1: Potentially Missing Composite Index\n**File**: `/workspace/flipt/internal/migrations/cockroachdb/20250121202326_create_table_counter_aggregated_analytics.up.sql`\n\n**Problem**:\nThe migration creates individual indexes but may not have an optimal composite index for query patterns:\n\n```sql\nCREATE INDEX idx_flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2_env_ns_flag \n    ON flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2 (environment_key, namespace_key, flag_key);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2_timestamp \n    ON flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2 (timestamp);\n```\n\nBoth queries filter on `(environment_key, namespace_key, flag_key, timestamp)`, but the indexes are separate. CockroachDB may use the first index and then filter on timestamp, or vice versa, resulting in suboptimal performance.\n\n**Impact**: **MEDIUM** - Queries will still work but may scan many unnecessary rows before filtering.\n\n**Recommendation**:\nAdd a composite index that covers the query's WHERE clause:\n\n```sql\n-- In the migration file, add:\nCREATE INDEX idx_flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2_env_ns_flag_ts \n    ON flipt_counter_aggregated_analytics_v2 (environment_key, namespace_key, flag_key, timestamp);\n```\n\nThis allows the database to jump directly to matching rows without filtering.\n\n---\n\n## 6. COMPARISON WITH CLICKHOUSE IMPLEMENTATION\n\nInterestingly, the ClickHouse implementation (`clickhouse/client.go`) has better efficiency patterns:\n\n1. **ClickHouse benefits**:\n   - Uses `fmt.Sprintf()` to dynamically build queries (not ideal for SQL injection, but shows flexibility)\n   - Better handling of pre-allocation using intermediate maps\n   - Has data downsampling logic (lines 269-273) for respecting limits\n   - Uses `WITH FILL` to guarantee uniform intervals in results\n\n2. **CockroachDB missing features**:\n   - No downsampling/result limiting per-flag\n   - No guaranteed uniform intervals (ClickHouse's `WITH FILL`)\n   - Weaker handling of large result sets\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| Issue | Severity | Impact | Category |\n|-------|----------|--------|----------|\n| Missing connection pool config | HIGH | Resource exhaustion, cascading failures | Connection Pooling |\n| Unbounded slice allocations | MEDIUM-HIGH | Large result set slow-downs, GC pressure | Memory |\n| Inefficient time bucketing | MEDIUM | Scales poorly with dataset size | Query Efficiency |\n| Missing LIMIT in single-flag query | MEDIUM | Unbounded memory usage | Query Efficiency |\n| Suboptimal batch query LIMIT | MEDIUM | Unnecessary aggregation work | Query Efficiency |\n| Inefficient result processing (copies) | LOW-MEDIUM | Memory overhead on large sets | Memory |\n| Redundant Ping() calls | LOW | Startup latency | Startup |\n| Blocking migrations in startup | MEDIUM | Deployment delays | Startup |\n| sync.Once error pattern | LOW | Maintainability risk | Code Quality |\n| Missing composite index | MEDIUM | Suboptimal query plans | Indexing |\n\n---\n\n## Immediate Action Items\n\n**Priority 1 (Critical)**:\n1.
